done_summary:
Produced three audience-tailored rewrites of the Friday outage memo:

1. **ceo_brief.md** (219/250 words) — Executive summary for board prep. Includes outage summary, impact scope (11,400 accounts, 28 enterprise, 4 downstream failures), second-occurrence-in-90-days framing, and four remediations. No engineering jargon, no named individuals, no informal apologies.

2. **customer_email.md** (268/350 words) — Warm, accountable mass email. Includes outage acknowledgment, plain-language root cause, four commitments for improvement, explicit SLA credit offer with contact path (CSM/support). No named engineer, no "second outage in 90 days" framing, no engineering jargon.

3. **legal_summary.md** (273/400 words) — Dispassionate factual summary for counsel. Includes exact incident window (14:11–18:24 UTC), scope breakdown, 99.9% SLA threshold with breach confirmation, four downstream-automation cases, and structured credit-request handling recommendation (contractual vs. relationship-driven). Uses "incident" not "outage." No named employees, no marketing language, no engineering remediation details.

All facts sourced exclusively from the draft memo. All word limits respected. All must_include/must_not_include constraints verified. ADR documents key omission decisions.
